title: Fused discounted-returns plus whitening kernel for REINFORCE++
summary: Integrate the existing returns_whiten Triton helper into the REINFORCE++ advantage path behind an auto|torch|triton switch, with benchmark and parity coverage.
success_gate: Match the existing REINFORCE++ returns-plus-masked_whiten semantics and beat the baseline on representative CUDA shapes before integrating into the trainer path.

results.integration: REINFORCE++ now uses returns_whiten on the auto path for validated float32 CUDA workloads; bfloat16 auto remains on the torch baseline.

notes: Tested with python -m unittest tests.test_returns_whiten_kernel -v and python -m unittest tests.test_advantage_kernels -v.
Explicit Triton helper parity was validated for bfloat16 against the float32 reference path, but auto does not enable it because the representative end-to-end bfloat16 benchmark did not win.
